requirements_conformance.evidence: Test spec 3 items all delivered: (1) shared fence-stripping helper closes forwarded F1; (2) fixture-document suite across pdf/email/ticket source types + first end-to-end shortlist->segment->execute_segment composition test; (3) optional live-Ollama smoke test with skipif reachability probe.

architecture_conformance.evidence: git diff 656b612..48f1845 -- engine/ proto/ is empty; scope correctly contained to agents/ingestion/. No new gRPC/proto surface introduced.

f1_closure_claim.evidence: Diffed propose_split.py against parent: change is import-swap + docstring only, zero logic/regex change (confirmed byte-identical _CODE_FENCE_RE and strip_code_fences body against the new _json_fences.py). segment.py has exactly one JSON-parsing code path (_parse_segment_json, single call site in segment()) and it calls strip_code_fences(raw) before json.loads on that path -- no other json.loads exists in the module. Adversarially tested strip_code_fences with: plain ```json fence, non-json language tag, leading/trailing whitespace outside the fence, no-fence input, single-line fence with no internal newlines, nested-fence-lookalike content inside the JSON string value -- all correctly stripped and parsed. Two adversarial cases DO break it: (a) multiple sequential fence blocks in one response, (b) prose text preceding/surrounding the fence (not a pure leading/trailing wrap). Both are pre-existing limitations inherited unchanged from propose_split.py's original implementation (disclosed there as 'a single leading/trailing fence'), not a new regression introduced by this commit, and outside the literal scope of what F1 described (single fence-wrapped JSON). Logged as new low-severity finding, non-blocking.

end_to_end_composition_test_claim.evidence: test_pipeline_shortlist_segment_wiring_end_to_end in test_segment_fixtures.py genuinely threads shortlist()'s real return value (shortlist_result) into segment()'s real argument, and segment()'s real return value (segment_result) into execute_segment()'s real argument -- verified by reading the test body: top_candidate/other_billing_candidate are derived from shortlist_result itself (not hand-built), the LLM's canned payload's target_topic is set to top_candidate.path (the real shortlist output), and execute_segment is called with segment_result directly. Confirmed via grep that no prior test file (test_shortlist.py, test_segment.py, test_segment_wiring.py) ever calls execute_segment with a segment()-derived SegmentResult or segment() with a shortlist()-derived candidate list -- each prior file only unit-tests its own module against hand-built payloads. The 'first end-to-end test in the repo' claim holds.

live_ollama_smoke_test_claim.evidence: _ollama_is_reachable() does a genuine short-timeout HTTP GET against Ollama's root endpoint, returns False on any httpx.HTTPError, True only on HTTP 200 -- not an always-True or trivially-skipped stub. Independently confirmed Ollama was actually running locally (ps aux showed ollama serve + llama-server process, curl to localhost:11434 returned 200) and personally re-ran agents/ingestion/test_segment_live.py: both tests (test_segment_against_real_ollama_model, test_propose_split_against_real_ollama_model) PASSED for real (not skipped), 14.22s wall time consistent with a real model call. The 'ran for real, not skipped' claim is independently verified, not taken on faith.

regression_claim_grpc_gencode.evidence: Ran full agents/ pytest suite at HEAD: 153 passed, 2 failed (test_grpc_client_translates_request_response, test_grpc_client_is_a_valid_search_candidates_fn_for_shortlist in test_shortlist.py), both failing with google.protobuf.runtime_version.VersionError (gencode 6.33.5 vs runtime 5.29.6 mismatch), an environment/toolchain issue unrelated to this diff. Independently bisected via git worktree at parent commit 656b612 (not stash, since working tree had no uncommitted tracked changes to stash) and reran the same two tests: identical failure, identical VersionError, at the parent commit. Confirms these are genuinely pre-existing and independent of this change, as claimed.

regression_risk.evidence: No production code path in segment.py changed other than the fence-stripping call site; existing test_segment.py/test_propose_split.py additions plus full suite rerun (153 passed, 2 pre-existing unrelated failures) show no new failures introduced.

test_coverage.evidence: Strong new coverage (fixture corpus across 3 source types, real end-to-end composition, live smoke test) but the shared strip_code_fences helper itself has no test for multiple-fence-block or prose-wrapped-fence inputs (see f1_closure_claim finding); not blocking since these are pre-existing propose_split.py limitations, not new gaps.

security.evidence: No new external input trust boundary; fence-stripping operates on already-authenticated-by-caller LLM completion strings, same trust boundary as before.

maintainability.evidence: Extraction correctly eliminates the two-copies-of-the-same-regex smell flagged as the root cause of F1; both call sites now share one implementation.

performance.evidence: No performance-relevant change; regex/parsing cost identical to before extraction.

confidence.evidence: All claims independently reproduced (diff inspection, adversarial fence inputs, grep for prior composition tests, live pytest run against a running Ollama instance, git-worktree bisection of the pre-existing failures).

findings.summary: strip_code_fences (shared, inherited unchanged from propose_split.py's original private implementation) only strips a single leading/trailing fence wrapping the ENTIRE response. It does not handle: (a) multiple sequential fence blocks in one completion, (b) prose text surrounding/preceding the fence rather than a pure whole-string wrap. Both cases, if a real Ollama model ever emits them, would still raise SegmentParseError/ProposeSplitParseError. Confirmed via direct adversarial testing of strip_code_fences(); not a new regression from this commit (identical behavior existed in propose_split.py before the extraction), and outside literal scope of forwarded finding F1 (which only described a single markdown-fence wrap).
findings.recommendation: If live-model observation ever shows multi-fence or prose-wrapped responses in practice, extend strip_code_fences to search for the first fenced block anywhere in the string rather than requiring a whole-string match; add regression tests for both cases.

tests_run_by_verifier.full_agents_suite: 153 passed, 2 failed (pre-existing, protobuf gencode/runtime mismatch, confirmed also failing at parent commit 656b612)
tests_run_by_verifier.live_ollama_smoke_test: 2 passed (genuinely executed against real local Ollama server, not skipped)
tests_run_by_verifier.ruff_agents_full_tree: 1 pre-existing F401 finding in generated agents/hivemind_pb2_grpc.py, confirmed untouched by this diff (not introduced by 48f1845)

issue_18_completion_assessment.caveat: Two escalation-required findings from earlier subtasks remain open and unresolved by design (F3: no real entity-index/edge-write gRPC exists in engine/proto despite 3.4.4's LLD-referenced acceptance criteria; F4: PutSegment's CREATE path never sets CatalogRecord.PathHash, pre-existing from task-3.2.2, blocks discoverability of newly created topics). These were previously disclosed and explicitly flagged as requiring a separate cross-cutting decision, not blocking this subtask, but the orchestrator should surface them to the user alongside any push/close authorization request for issue #18 so the user decides with full information, not silently.
